## Tier

1

## Branch

dev

## Problem

`docs/CASE_AUDIT_2026-05-18.md` is a static point-in-time snapshot (11 cases, dated 2026-05-18) now
representing less than 15% of the current case inventory (85+ cases). It remains marked `status:
canonical` despite being 3+ weeks stale, causing operators to treat it as live truth. The `CASE_DASHBOARD.md`
provides Dataview-powered live equivalents but lacks an executive summary section.

## Contrarian Review

> [!warning] RETROACTIVE — workflow was bypassed
> This case file was created after implementation (commit c33e905 on 2026-06-14). The
> Contrarian phase was not run before work started — this is a process violation. See
> [[plt-case-obsidian-orchestration-wiring]] for the governance fix that prevents recurrence.

**Tier: 1** — pure docs, no code, no contracts, no state machine, no auth.
**Scope: platform** — all files in `docs/` and `docs/cases/`.
**Cross-app risk: none** — no app code or contracts touched.
**Simplest path:** archive the static audit + add Dataview to dashboard. Confirmed correct approach.

## Success Criterion

`docs/CASE_AUDIT_2026-05-18.md` displays a deprecation callout in Obsidian; `CASE_DASHBOARD.md`
renders an "At a glance" counts table as the first section; `vault-hygiene.ps1` passes with no
broken wikilinks; no other doc references the audit file as current truth.

## Investigation

- `CASE_AUDIT_2026-05-18.md`: `status: canonical`, `date:` key (not `last_reviewed:`), 11-case snapshot.
  Executioner verdict from `plt-case-app-audit-platform-docs` explicitly listed this file as a
  follow-up cleanup target (Approved 2026-05-20).
- `CASE_DASHBOARD.md`: had "Counts by status" only at the bottom — no executive summary at top.
- `DOCS_HUB.md`: linked audit under "Business / audits" (live section), not under "Archive".
- `obsidian-vault-hygiene.md`: Tier C section had no guidance on audit-type docs being point-in-time.

## Root Cause

The `plt-case-app-audit-platform-docs` Executioner verdict (2026-05-20) flagged the stale audit as a
follow-up but no case file was created for the cleanup. The work sat unactioned for 3+ weeks.

## Proposed Fix

1. Archive `CASE_AUDIT_2026-05-18.md` — add deprecation callout; update frontmatter to `status: archived` + `last_reviewed:`.
2. Promote CASE_DASHBOARD "At a glance" — move "Counts by status" to top; add total-count query.
3. Reroute DOCS_HUB — move audit link from "Business / audits" to "Archive (historical)".
4. Add Tier C guidance to `obsidian-vault-hygiene.md` — audits are point-in-time; dashboard is live.

## Scribe Implementation

**Files changed (all in commit c33e905 on branch dev):**

- `docs/CASE_AUDIT_2026-05-18.md` — `status: archived`; added `last_reviewed: 2026-05-18`; deprecation callout pointing to `[[CASE_DASHBOARD]]`.
- `docs/cases/CASE_DASHBOARD.md` — new "At a glance" section at top with counts-by-status Dataview + total-count query; removed duplicate "Counts by status" section at bottom.
- `docs/DOCS_HUB.md` — audit link moved from "Business / audits" to "Archive (historical)" with description note.
- `docs/obsidian-vault-hygiene.md` — Tier C table appended with note: "CASE_DASHBOARD is the live view; full audits are point-in-time Tier C scribe tasks."

**Code Simplification:** SKIPPED — pure docs, no code paths.

## Verification

Run the following and confirm all pass:

1. `grep -r "CASE_AUDIT_2026-05-18" docs/ --include="*.md"` — only archive section of DOCS_HUB and the file itself should match (no live-section links).
2. `.\scripts\vault-hygiene.ps1` — Tier A broken-links check must pass (0 broken wikilinks).
3. Open `CASE_AUDIT_2026-05-18.md` in Obsidian — deprecation callout must be visible at top.
4. Open `CASE_DASHBOARD.md` in Obsidian — "At a glance" section must render a live counts table as the first section (requires Dataview active).
